introSection: Primordial is a steel roller coaster operating at Lagoon in Farmington, Utah, USA. The ride includes interactive elements provided by Canadian company Triotech.

sections.title: History
sections.content: Construction started on Primordial in 2018, with leaked rumors of its name from a trademarked filed from Lagoon. In September 2019, the park revealed this ride would have a footprint 30% larger than that of Cannibal. The ride opened to the public on September 15, 2023.

sections.title: Design
sections.content:
Primordial has 2 cars per train, each seating a max of 8 riders. Each car features onboard audio, as well as a controlled rotation, facing towards the screens in its dark-ride portions, and backwards during its second coaster portion.

The finale of the ride consists of three possible endings, a vertical drop track, and a backwards or forwards drop.

fullText:
Primordial is a steel roller coaster operating at Lagoon in Farmington, Utah, USA. The ride includes interactive elements provided by Canadian company Triotech.

Construction started on Primordial in 2018, with leaked rumors of its name from a trademarked filed from Lagoon. In September 2019, the park revealed this ride would have a footprint 30% larger than that of Cannibal. The ride opened to the public on September 15, 2023.

Primordial has 2 cars per train, each seating a max of 8 riders. Each car features onboard audio, as well as a controlled rotation, facing towards the screens in its dark-ride portions, and backwards during its second coaster portion.

The finale of the ride consists of three possible endings, a vertical drop track, and a backwards or forwards drop.
